failure_summary: The parent reward still over-rewards early_failure trajectories via approach_progress and terminal_reward components, and does not generate high-confidence success_like behavior.

main_useful_patterns: approach_progress, proximity_bonus, near_stability, leg_bonus favor higher-quality trajectories
uncertainties: No high-confidence success_like trajectory exists; preference construction forbidden
Label margin sufficient but decision_level remains no_decision
Component_summary includes components not in parent code, complicating diagnosis

component_diagnosis.evidence: Repeated archival lessons indicate approach_progress over-rewards early_failure; component_summary shows failure_mean (1.03) and positive_mean (1.73), with insufficient gap.
Component_summary shows failure_mean (4.375) higher than positive_mean (2.994), indicating failures receive unwarranted positive terminal reward; archival lessons confirm misassignment.
Component_summary shows positive_mean (18.21) much higher than failure_mean (2.27), indicating good discrimination.
Component_summary shows positive_mean (24.71) much higher than failure_mean (4.23), indicating strong favor for better trajectories.
Component_summary shows nearly all reward goes to positives (failure_mean=0, positive_mean=23.07).
Penalty is small and has little impact; no strong signal to change.

forbidden_changes: do not add global survival bonus without progress gating
do not only scale all coefficients
do not treat partial_progress as success_like
required_changes: restructure approach_progress to penalize early_failure and widen margin to partial_progress (e.g., add quality-based scaling or negative bias)
remove or gate terminal_reward to prevent rewarding non-landing terminations
ensure any new terminal reward only activates for confirmed safe landings

rollback_decision.reason: Parent reward shows positive training return (6.04) and subsequent rounds have not consistently outperformed; no clear regression warranting rollback.

self_evaluation_lesson: Partial_or_success_only coverage prevents strong preference construction; recurrent problem is approach_progress rewarding early_failure, requiring structural quality-based scaling rather than coefficient-only adjustments; terminal_reward must be removed or gated to avoid rewarding crashes.
